visa_types.name: Business
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).
A printout of the confirmation of registration from the Application Portal
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Sign cover letter received by email after registering application on Application Portal.
Checklist and the documents listed on it ( Business )
Travel Document (and copies of used pages)
Travel document should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area.
Photograph
1 photograph in line with ICAO guidelines.
Travel Medical Insurance
Travel medical insurance must cover the entire period of intended stay, be valid for all Schengen States and cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30 000.
Copies of previous visas (if you had any)
Birth certificate, marriage certificate and certificate on joint household.
Proof of occupation
If you are employed:
- Employment contract (certificate of business registration for owners of private enterprises) – translated into English
- Permission for leave – translated into English
- Bank statement for last three months.
If you are student:
- Certificate from university/school translated into English.
If you are retired:
- Pension certificate translated into English.
For persons under 18 years old:
- Birth certificate
- Valid passport (and copy of passport)
- Copy of parents passports
- Letter of consent signed by both parents, authorizing travel to Norway, translated into English language

Business
- Signed invitation letter from the Norwegian/Danish/Icelandic company/organization written on official company paper, containing the following information:
- Contact details of the company
- Name and date of birth and passport details of the invited person
- Purpose, destination and duration of the visit
- Detailed programme
- Clarification who will cover the travel costs of the invited person
- Name and position of the countersigning officer
- Letter in English from the Kosovo company about the purpose of the trip and the nature of the connection with the Norwegian part
- Business registration
- Bank statement
- Companies turnover for the last tax year
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time for these may be up to 30 days. In exceptional cases UDI may take up to 60 days to make a decision.
In Kosovo, you will in most cases get the result of your application within 15 calendar days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Ankara. Please note: that you should account for an additional 4 days for sending the application documents between the VFS application center in Prishtina and Ankara.
visa_types.forms: - The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal  https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/)
visa_types.overview:
Visa Fees
Most applicants will have to pay an application fee. Here you can find an overview of how much you will have to pay, how to pay and refund.
Fees - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
Please Note: There is a service charge levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Service Fee: Euro 26.50 (Inclusive of VAT)
- Resubmission Fee: Euro 22.50 ( Inclusive of VAT)
- Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years old
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests, other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
visa_types.full_text:
## Visa Fees
Most applicants will have to pay an application fee. Here you can find an overview of how much you will have to pay, how to pay and refund.
Fees - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
Please Note: There is a service charge levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Service Fee: Euro 26.50 (Inclusive of VAT)
- Resubmission Fee: Euro 22.50 ( Inclusive of VAT)
- Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years old
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests, other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).
A printout of the confirmation of registration from the Application Portal
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Sign cover letter received by email after registering application on Application Portal.
Checklist and the documents listed on it ( Business )
Travel Document (and copies of used pages)
Travel document should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area.
Photograph
1 photograph in line with ICAO guidelines.
Travel Medical Insurance
Travel medical insurance must cover the entire period of intended stay, be valid for all Schengen States and cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30 000.
Copies of previous visas (if you had any)
Birth certificate, marriage certificate and certificate on joint household.
Proof of occupation
If you are employed:
- Employment contract (certificate of business registration for owners of private enterprises) – translated into English
- Permission for leave – translated into English
- Bank statement for last three months.
If you are student:
- Certificate from university/school translated into English.
If you are retired:
- Pension certificate translated into English.
For persons under 18 years old:
- Birth certificate
- Valid passport (and copy of passport)
- Copy of parents passports
- Letter of consent signed by both parents, authorizing travel to Norway, translated into English language

## Documents needed from person/ host in Norway/Denmark/Iceland
Business
- Signed invitation letter from the Norwegian/Danish/Icelandic company/organization written on official company paper, containing the following information:
- Contact details of the company
- Name and date of birth and passport details of the invited person
- Purpose, destination and duration of the visit
- Detailed programme
- Clarification who will cover the travel costs of the invited person
- Name and position of the countersigning officer
- Letter in English from the Kosovo company about the purpose of the trip and the nature of the connection with the Norwegian part
- Business registration
- Bank statement
- Companies turnover for the last tax year

## Photo Specifications
- 1 Photograph in line with ICAO guidelines
.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time for these may be up to 30 days. In exceptional cases UDI may take up to 60 days to make a decision.
In Kosovo, you will in most cases get the result of your application within 15 calendar days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Ankara. Please note: that you should account for an additional 4 days for sending the application documents between the VFS application center in Prishtina and Ankara.

## Download Forms
- The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal  https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/)

visa_types.name: Residence Permit
visa_types.overview:
Residence Permit
For Norway, all permits that require a stay for longer than 90 days are called residence permits. The grounds for applying a residence permit can be work, family reunion, studies etc.
Please note that if you plan to work in Norway you will, as a main rule, need a residence permit even if the stay is planned to be shorter than 90 days.
To read about the different residence permits and requirements please see the webpages of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/). By entering general information about your nationality and the reason for applying you will get specific information on what documents to present, case processing times etc.
visa_types.full_text:
## Residence Permit
For Norway, all permits that require a stay for longer than 90 days are called residence permits. The grounds for applying a residence permit can be work, family reunion, studies etc.
Please note that if you plan to work in Norway you will, as a main rule, need a residence permit even if the stay is planned to be shorter than 90 days.
To read about the different residence permits and requirements please see the webpages of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (http://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/). By entering general information about your nationality and the reason for applying you will get specific information on what documents to present, case processing times etc.

visa_types.name: Tourist
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).
A printout of the confirmation of registration from the Application Portal
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Sign cover letter received by email after registering application on Application Portal.
Checklist and the documents listed on it ( Tourist – Visiting Family& Friends )
Travel Document (and copies of used pages)
Travel document should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area.
Photograph
1 photograph in line with ICAO guidelines.
Travel Medical Insurance
Travel medical insurance must cover the entire period of intended stay, be valid for all Schengen States and cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30 000.
Proof of sufficient funds for the entire stay
Bank account statement of the last 3 months
Hotel reservation or other proof of accommodation
Other documents considered necessary by the applicant
Copies of previous visas (if you had any)
Birth certificate, marriage certificate certificate on joint household and if you are visiting close family: you must document existing family ties (ex: their birth certificate).
Proof of occupation
- Employed
Contract of employment
Vacation confirmation from the employer
Evidence of payment of contributions to the Kosovo Pension Savings (Trusti) over the last 6 months
Self-employed/Entrepreneur
Certificate of registration of business
Company’s bank statement covering the last 3 months
Certification of payment of taxes (VAT FILER)
Farmer
Proof of property ownership
Bank statement of the farm covering the last 3 months
Certification of payment of taxes (VAT FILER)
Unemployed/housewife
Proof of the sufficient funds for the entire stay, income of household family member who supports you financially; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
Student
Valid student card
Confirmation letter of regular presence from the university/school signed and stamped by the university
Copy of both parents’ identity cards/passports (for minors)
Proof of the sufficient funds for the entire stay, income of household family member who supports you financially; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
If you are retired:
- Pension certificate translated into English.
Minor (under the age 18 travelling alone or with one parent/legal guardian)
Written consent of both guardians/parents and copies of their passports
Single parent must prove sole guardianship
Parent's income; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
If accompanied by other adult – copy of a passport and visa of that person
- Guarantee form from the inviting part in Norway(not older than 3 months). MUST BE ORIGINAL
signed and stamped by the police in Norway
specifying the dates and duration of the trip
specifying the relation between the applicant and the host person
For travelers to Denmark guarantee form VU2, not older than 3 months
Invitation letter for travelers to Norway as well to
Denmark and Iceland
(not older than 3 months)
dated and signed by the host
specifying the purpose and duration of the trip
including details of the inviting and invited person
mentioning if the guest is a friend/spouse/relative
copy of the inviters passport and valid residence permit (for not Norwegian citizens) are required
For travelers to Iceland -Invitation letters need to be notarized in Iceland

Please note: that you should account for an additional 4 days for sending the application documents between the VFS application center in Prishtina and Ankara.

visa_types.name: Others
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).
A printout of the confirmation of registration from the Application Portal
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Sign cover letter received by email after registering application on Application Portal.
Checklist and the documents listed on it ( Business, Tourist – Visiting Family& Friends, Sport – Culture-Medical)
Travel Document (and copies of used pages)
Travel document should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area.
Photograph
1 photograph in line with ICAO guidelines.
Travel Medical Insurance
Travel medical insurance must cover the entire period of intended stay, be valid for all Schengen States and cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30 000.
Copies of previous visas (if you had any)
Birth certificate, marriage certificate and certificate on joint household.

If you are employed:

For persons under 18 years old:

- Valid passport (and copy of passport)
- Copy of parents passports
- Letter of consent signed by both parents, authorizing travel to Norway, translated into English language

Visit family member/ friend
- Original “Guarantee form for visit (https://www.udi.no/globalassets/global/skjemaer/garantiskjema-besok-guarantee-form-visit-gp7018-be.pdf)” stamped by Norwegian authorities.
The guarantee can't be more than 3 months old.
- Signed invitation (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/invitation-for-a-visitors-visa-/) from family member/friend
- Copy of the guarantor’s passport
Business
- Invitation in English from Norwegian company showing the company’s organization number and stating the purpose and length of the visit
- Letter in English from the Kosovo company about the purpose of the trip and the nature of the connection with the Norwegian part
- Business registration
- Bank statement
- Companies turnover for the last tax year

visa_types.name: Study
visa_types.documents_required:
Applicants must submit the documents as per the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) student checklist (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/studietillatelse/?c=xxk)
Applicants must also submit the documents listed here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/studietillatelse/?c=xxk).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application)and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in  (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/)Pristina.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy inAnkara, which is responsible for the further examination
visa_types.visa_fees:

Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of: 26.50 EUR and Resubmission Fee of: 22.50 EUR per application, which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application center. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.
Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
The following are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Family Immigration residence permit:

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to study in Norway, you must apply for a residence permit for studies. This is also called a study permit. You can apply for a residence permit to study at a university college or university, attend bible school or folk high school. You can also apply for a study permit if you are a skilled worker and are going to study Norwegian or take education to be recognized in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Pristina (https://www.udi.no/en/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in,Ankara which is responsible for the further examination.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you wish to study in Norway, you must apply for a residence permit for studies. This is also called a study permit. You can apply for a residence permit to study at a university college or university, attend bible school or folk high school. You can also apply for a study permit if you are a skilled worker and are going to study Norwegian or take education to be recognized in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/) and The Norwegian Embassy in Pristina (https://www.udi.no/en/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in,Ankara which is responsible for the further examination.

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the visa fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Please note that applicants will also be charged a service fee of: 26.50 EUR and Resubmission Fee of: 22.50 EUR per application, which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application center. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.
Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
The following are exempted from the fee when submitting an application for Family Immigration residence permit:
- Children under 18 years of age
- Close family members of EU/EEA nationals, who apply for a residence card.
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).

## Documents Required
Applicants must submit the documents as per the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) student checklist (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/studietillatelse/?c=xxk)
Applicants must also submit the documents listed here (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/studietillatelse/?c=xxk).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/checklists-which-explains-which-documents-you-must-hand-in-with-your-application)and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in  (https://www.norway.no/en/united-arab-emirates/)Pristina.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy inAnkara, which is responsible for the further examination

## Photo Specifications
The photo must:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35–40 mm in width
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
For the latest information on processing times in Study Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI). (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/studies/)

## Download Forms
The application form for a Norway visa must be completed online. To launch an online application, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).

visa_types.name: Visiting Family and Friends
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).
A printout of the confirmation of registration from the Application Portal
The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Sign cover letter received by email after registering application on Application Portal.
Checklist and the documents listed on it ( Tourist – Visiting Family and Friends)
Travel Document (and copies of used pages)
Travel document should be valid for at least 3 months after the intended date of (last) departure from the Schengen area.
Photograph
1 photograph in line with ICAO guidelines.
Travel Medical Insurance
Travel medical insurance must cover the entire period of intended stay, be valid for all Schengen States and cover any expenses which might arise in connection with repatriation for medical reasons, urgency medical attention, emergency hospital treatment or death during the stay. The minimum coverage must be €30 000.
Proof of sufficient funds for the entire stay
Bank account statement of the last 3 months
Hotel reservation or other proof of accommodation
Other documents considered necessary by the applicant
Copies of previous visas (if you had any)
Birth certificate, marriage certificate certificate on joint household.and if you are visiting close family: you must document existing family ties (ex: their birth certificate).
Proof of occupation
- Employed
Contract of employment
Vacation confirmation from the employer
Evidence of payment of contributions to the Kosovo Pension Savings (Trusti) over the last 6 months
Self-employed/Entrepreneur
Certificate of registration of business
Company’s bank statement covering the last 3 months
Certification of payment of taxes (VAT FILER)
Farmer
Proof of property ownership
Bank statement of the farm covering the last 3 months
Certification of payment of taxes (VAT FILER)
Unemployed/housewife
Proof of the sufficient funds for the entire stay, income of household family member who supports you financially; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
Student
Valid student card
Confirmation letter of regular presence from the university/school signed and stamped by the university
Copy of both parents’ identity cards/passports (for minors)
Proof of the sufficient funds for the entire stay, income of household family member who supports you financially; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
If you are retired:
- Pension certificate translated into English.
Minor (under the age 18 travelling alone or with one parent/legal guardian)
Written consent of both guardians/parents and copies of their passports
Single parent must prove sole guardianship
Parent's income; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
If accompanied by other adult – copy of a passport and visa of that person
- Guarantee form from the inviting part in Norway(not older than 3 months). MUST BE ORIGINAL
signed and stamped by the police in Norway
specifying the dates and duration of the trip
specifying the relation between the applicant and the host person
For travelers to Denmark guarantee form VU2, not older than 3 months
Invitation letter for travelers to Norway as well to
Denmark and Iceland
(not older than 3 months)
dated and signed by the host
specifying the purpose and duration of the trip
including details of the inviting and invited person
mentioning if the guest is a friend/spouse/relative
copy of the inviters passport and valid residence permit (for not Norwegian citizens) are required
For travelers to Iceland -Invitation letters need to be notarized in Iceland
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time for these may be up to 30 days. In exceptional cases UDI may take up to 60 days to make a decision.
In Kosovo, you will in most cases get the result of your application within 15 calendar days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Ankara. Please note: that you should account for an additional 4 days for sending the application documents between the VFS application center in Prishtina and Ankara.
visa_types.forms: - The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal  https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/)
visa_types.overview:
Visa Fees
Most applicants will have to pay an application fee. Here you can find an overview of how much you will have to pay, how to pay and refund.
Fees - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
Please Note: There is a service charge levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Service Fee: Euro 26.50 (Inclusive of VAT)
- Resubmission Fee: Euro 22.50 ( Inclusive of VAT)
- Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years old
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests, other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.
visa_types.full_text:
## Visa Fees
Most applicants will have to pay an application fee. Here you can find an overview of how much you will have to pay, how to pay and refund.
Fees - UDI (https://www.udi.no/en/word-definitions/fees/#link-3598)
Please Note: There is a service charge levied per application over and above the visa fees.
- Service Fee: Euro 26.50 (Inclusive of VAT)
- Resubmission Fee: Euro 22.50 ( Inclusive of VAT)
- Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Applicants exempted from paying the Visa Fee:
- Children under 6 years old
- School pupils, students, post-graduate students and accompanying teachers who undertake trips for the purpose of study or educational training
- Researchers from third countries travelling for the purpose of carrying out scientific research, as per specific rules
- Applicants related to (spouse, descendant or dependent ascendant of) EEA citizen who are exercising their right to free movement (on production of relevant certificates (birth certificate, marriage certificate, etc.)
In certain cases, the fee may be waived or reduced in accordance with national legislation, when this would serve to promote cultural, foreign policy or development policy interests, other areas of vital public interest, or for humanitarian reasons.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/xkx/en/nor/travel-insurance).

The minimum coverage must be €30 000.

If you are retired:
- Pension certificate translated into English.
Minor (under the age 18 travelling alone or with one parent/legal guardian)
Written consent of both guardians/parents and copies of their passports
Single parent must prove sole guardianship
Parent's income; work contract, bank statement for the last 3 months
If accompanied by other adult – copy of a passport and visa of that person

copy of the inviters passport and valid residence permit (for not Norwegian citizens) are required
For travelers to Iceland -Invitation letters need to be notarized in Iceland

## Photo Specifications
- 1 Photograph in line with ICAO guidelines
.

## Processing Time
The processing time for applications is normally up to 15 calendar days. In some cases, applications are sent to the Directorate of Immigration (UDI), and the processing time for these may be up to 30 days. In exceptional cases UDI may take up to 60 days to make a decision.
In Kosovo, you will in most cases get the result of your application within 15 calendar days from the date the application is received at the Embassy in Ankara. Please note: that you should account for an additional 4 days for sending the application documents between the VFS application center in Prishtina and Ankara.

## Download Forms
- The applicant can register the visa application online on the Application Portal  https://selfservice.udi.no/ (https://selfservice.udi.no/)

Overview

## Overview
If you wish to come to Norway to work, you need a residence permit. You must normally have found a job first. What residence permit you should apply for depends on your competence and the type of work you will be doing in Norway.
Please click on each of the tabs above for more information or visit the websites of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=xxk) and The Norwegian Embassy in Pristina (https://www.udi.no/en/).
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in,Ankara which is responsible for the further examination.

## Visa Fees
When you apply for a visa or residence permit to Norway, you must pay the fee online when registering your application at the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
Please note that applicants will also be charged a Service Fee of: 26.50 EUR and Resubmission Fee of: 22.50 EUR per application, which must be paid in cash when you attend the visa application center. The service fee is liable to change in line with the European Commission exchange rate.
Courier re-submission: The applicant may request to have the passport returned from the embassy while the application is being processed. If the applicant later resubmits the passport for issuance of visa, VFS may request the applicant to pay 22.50 EUR to cover the Resubmission Courier.
Visa fees can only be paid with a credit card through the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).

## Documents Required
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI)  (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=xxk)and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Pristina (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/work-immigration/?c=xxk).
Please be advised that it is a requirement that all documents are submitted in original in the A4 format. Any government issued documents that you provide originals for (birth certificates etc) must be accompanied by a copy. Any documents not in A4 size need to be copied into A4. The copies will have to be of very good quality with no staples, paper clips or glue as they will be scanned by the Embassy.
We stress the importance of submitting all the relevant documents. Missing documents may lead to a rejection or longer processing time. If you for any reason are not able to submit any of the documents, please write a short statement explaining this.
Kindly note that VFS will send the application by courier mail to the Norwegian Embassy in -Ankara, which is responsible for the further examination

## Photo Specifications
The photo must:
- be in sharp focus, clear and with good contrasts
- be printed on high quality photo paper with high solution
- be in colour
- be 35–40 mm in width
- be maximum 6 months old and resemble the applicant
- be taken with the applicant facing the camera directly, against a light background
- show the whole head and the top of the shoulders. The face must occupy 70–80 % of the picture.
- show open eyes that are clearly visible. Hair must not cover the eyes.
Regarding glasses:
Glasses are allowed as long as the eyes are clearly visible.
- the glasses must not be coloured
- the rim must not cover any part of the eyes
- there must be no reflection in the glasses
Regarding head-dress:
Religious head-dresses are allowed, provided that all details of the face are clearly shown.
- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
For the latest information on processing times in Family Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/).

## Download Forms
The application form for a Norway visa must be completed online. To launch an online application, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).

visa_types.documents_required:
For information about the documents required, please visit the website of the Norway Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/) and go to the website of The Norwegian Embassy in Pristina (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/).

- Questions about the relationship between spouses from EU in family immigration cases

- chin, forehead and both cheeks must be clearly visible
- shadows on the face due to the head-dress are not allowed

Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete. A photo booth for meeting these requirements is available at the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
For the latest information on processing times in Family Immigration applications, please go to the website of the Norwegian Directorate of Immigration (UDI) (https://www.udi.no/en/want-to-apply/family-immigration/family-immigration-with-a-citizen-of-a-country-outside-the-eueea/?resetguide=1).

## Download Forms
The application form for a Norway visa must be completed online. To launch an online application, please visit the Application Portal Norway (https://selfservice.udi.no/).
